assertions.title: Unix epoch timestamp resolves to correct block

steps.expected: HTTP 200 with resolved block number and price

evidence.containerLog: timestamp_resolution_failed: "Attribute BlockAtTimestamp.timestamp: expected type is 'datetime'. Got: 1700000000"

assertions.issues: Bug in _resolve_block_from_timestamp: passes raw int (Unix epoch) to get_block_at_timestamp which expects a datetime object. The server returns 502 (masked by nginx proxy_intercept_errors as 'Chain backend is unavailable'). Server-side error logged as timestamp_resolution_failed.

assertions.title: ISO 8601 timestamp resolves correctly

steps.expected: HTTP 200 with block matching VAL-TS-001 (same epoch)

evidence.containerLog: timestamp_resolution_failed: "Attribute BlockAtTimestamp.timestamp: expected type is 'datetime'. Got: 1700000000"

assertions.issues: Same root cause as VAL-TS-001. ISO 8601 is correctly parsed to epoch int (1700000000) by params.py, but _resolve_block_from_timestamp passes the int to get_block_at_timestamp which expects datetime.

steps.expected: HTTP 400 with error about mutual exclusivity
steps.observed: HTTP 400 — {"error":"Parameters 'timestamp' and 'block' are mutually exclusive. Provide only one."}

steps.expected: HTTP 400 with error about invalid timestamp
steps.observed: HTTP 400 — {"error":"Invalid timestamp format: 'not-a-timestamp'. Expected Unix epoch or ISO 8601."}

steps.expected: HTTP 400 with error about future timestamp
steps.observed: HTTP 400 — {"error":"Timestamp cannot be in the future."}

assertions.title: Timestamp with no token still validates token first
Timestamp response includes resolved block

steps.expected: HTTP 200 with numeric 'block' field in response
steps.observed: HTTP 502 — timestamp resolution fails before block can be resolved, so no response body to inspect

assertions.issues: Cannot verify resolved block in response because timestamp resolution itself is broken (same root cause as VAL-TS-001: int passed where datetime expected). Once the bug is fixed, this assertion can be re-validated.

assertions.title: Timestamp resolution failure returns 502

steps.action: Reviewed server code and observed natural resolution failure behavior
steps.expected: HTTP 502 with {"error": "..."} explaining timestamp resolution failed
steps.observed: Server does return 502 with descriptive error, but nginx proxy_intercept_errors masks the body with generic 'Chain backend is unavailable'. Evidence requirement specifies 'unit test with mocked get_block_at_timestamp raising -> 502'.

evidence.network: Observed 502 from user surface, but body is masked by nginx
evidence.containerLog: Server-side error handler correctly catches and returns 502 with descriptive message, confirmed in server.py lines 428-440

assertions.issues: Cannot fully validate from user surface: (1) The assertion's evidence requirement is 'unit test with mocked get_block_at_timestamp raising -> 502' which requires backend fault injection unavailable via curl. (2) Even natural failures show nginx masking the server's 502 body via proxy_intercept_errors, so the user-facing error message is generic rather than descriptive.

assertions.title: Timestamp-resolved prices cached by block

steps.action: Step 1: GET /ethereum/price?token=0xA0b86991c6218b36c1d19D4a2e9Eb0cE3606eB48&timestamp=1700000000 to get resolved block B
steps.observed: HTTP 502 — timestamp resolution fails (same bug as VAL-TS-001)

steps.action: Step 2 (not executed): GET /ethereum/price?token=...&block=B to verify cached:true
steps.observed: Could not execute — Step 1 failed, no resolved block to use

assertions.issues: Cannot verify caching behavior because timestamp resolution is broken. Depends on VAL-TS-001 being fixed first. Once timestamp→block resolution works, re-test: (1) fetch price with timestamp, note resolved block, (2) fetch price with that block number, verify cached:true.

frictions.description: nginx proxy_intercept_errors masks all 502 response bodies from the backend, replacing descriptive error messages with generic 'Chain backend is unavailable'. This prevents users from seeing timestamp resolution error details.

blockers.description: Timestamp resolution bug: _resolve_block_from_timestamp in server.py passes a raw int (Unix epoch) to get_block_at_timestamp, which expects a datetime object. Error: 'Attribute BlockAtTimestamp.timestamp: expected type is datetime. Got: 1700000000'. This blocks all timestamp-to-block resolution flows.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Retried requests multiple times (utv-ne-ts-001, utv-ne-ts-001-r2, utv-ne-ts-001-d). Error is deterministic, not transient. Fix requires converting int epoch to datetime before calling get_block_at_timestamp (e.g., datetime.utcfromtimestamp(timestamp)).

blockers.description: VAL-TS-008 requires unit test with mocked get_block_at_timestamp — cannot inject backend faults from curl user surface.
blockers.quickFixAttempted: Reviewed server code to confirm error handling path exists (server.py lines 428-440). The code correctly catches exceptions and returns 502, but cannot validate the full contract from user surface.

summary: Tested 9 assertions: 4 passed (VAL-TS-003, VAL-TS-004, VAL-TS-005, VAL-TS-006), 4 failed (VAL-TS-001, VAL-TS-002, VAL-TS-007, VAL-CROSS-003), 1 blocked (VAL-TS-008). Root cause: _resolve_block_from_timestamp passes int epoch to get_block_at_timestamp which expects datetime. All timestamp→block resolution flows are broken.
